scenario: Lattice Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with 4 clusters and routes 7000 shipments per day through a DRD that bundles several decision tables and literal expressions. An operator opens the Decisions page in Operate, filters by name and version, and deletes one decision definition from that DRD.
question: Which other resources inside the same DRD does this single deletion remove?

options.text: Every other decision table inside the DRD is kept, but the literal expressions belonging to the deleted definition are removed
Every other decision table and literal expression that is part of the same DRD is removed alongside the selected decision definition
Every other decision table inside the DRD is moved to a new DRD, while only literal expressions of the deleted definition are removed
Every other decision table and literal expression inside the DRD is kept and re-linked to the most recent surviving decision definition

a.text: Incorrect. The Decisions page docs say every other decision table in the same DRD is also deleted; scoping the removal to only literal expressions of the picked definition does not match the documented impact.

b.text: Correct. The Decisions page docs state that deleting a decision definition deletes the DRD, removing all other decision tables and literal expressions that are part of the same DRD alongside the selected definition.

c.text: Incorrect. The delete operation does not move resources to a new DRD; the entire DRD that contained the picked definition is removed in one step, including its other decision tables and literal expressions.

d.text: Incorrect. Surviving resources are not re-linked to another definition; the docs say the whole DRD goes away, so the other decision tables and literal expressions are removed with it.

explanation: On the Decisions page, deleting a decision definition removes the DRD it belongs to, taking every other decision table and literal expression that is part of that DRD with it in the same operation.
